name: Cusano v. Klein
summary: The Ninth Circuit held that a debtor's post-petition copyright royalty claims were not property of the estate because they arose from post-petition creative work, even though the copyrights predated the filing.
significance: A circuit court decision often cited for the proposition that causes of action that are sufficiently 'rooted' in pre-petition conduct become property of the estate, illustrating the circuit split on this issue.
